summary: Excellent audience recording captures a high‑energy show with standout Friend of the Devil, 1/2 Step > Franklin’s Tower and an explosive Other One.
review: Both reviewers praise the performance – a short but punchy first set followed by a second set that roars with strong Stranger, a soaring Friend of the Devil, tight 1/2 Step > Franklin’s, and a thunderous Other One that even blew a speaker. The audience tape delivers a vivid “you are there” theatre feel; although one reviewer notes early muffling and echo, the sound improves and is generally regarded as superior to the available soundboard source.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is consistently rated high (energetic set, many standout songs) while recording quality is mostly praised with minor early muffling; combined weighted score yields ~4.3 stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Solid theatre ambience with a strong 'you are there' feel; some reviewers note the recording is muffled and echo‑y at the very start but it clears up quickly.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A brief, high‑energy first set that sets the mood, followed by a second set that builds momentum with tight transitions and powerful jams, ending on a satisfying Brokedown.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Strong vocal delivery; occasional lyrical slip during Crazy Fingers but recovers with a soaring outro jam.
band_member_comments.Phil: Delivers an aggressive bass attack on The Other One that blew a speaker, showcasing his full‑on power.
band_member_comments.Drums: Highlights include a fantastic drum segment after the Crazy Fingers jam and overall driving rhythmic energy.
